title: FMD-001: Rapid Play/Pause Button Presses — Tone Stacking Detection on Sync 20
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and ensure Sync 20 is connected via Bluetooth.
Play streaming audio (e.g., Spotify, YouTube Music) at 50% volume.
On the physical Sync 20 device, press the Play/Pause button rapidly 10 times within 3 seconds.
Observe audio output for tone stacking (multiple overlapping tones) or missing tones.
Attempt to play/pause audio normally using the Sync 20 Play/Pause button.
If audio is unresponsive or distorted, power off the Sync 20 by pressing and holding the Power button for 2 seconds, then power back on.
Reconnect to Sync 20 via Poly Studio Mobile and attempt normal Play/Pause playback.
expected: No tone stacking or missing tones occur during or after rapid Play/Pause presses. Audio playback responds correctly to subsequent normal Play/Pause presses. If audio becomes unresponsive, power cycling the Sync 20 restores normal Play/Pause functionality without further issues.

title: FMD-002: Rapid Play/Pause Button Presses — Tone Stacking Detection on Voyager Series
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and ensure Voyager (e.g., Voyager 5200, Voyager 4320) is connected via Bluetooth.
Start media playback (music or podcast) on the connected phone.
On the physical Voyager device, rapidly press the Play/Pause button 15 times within 5 seconds.
Listen carefully for overlapping tones, stuttering, or missing audio cues (FMD tones).
After the rapid presses, press Play/Pause once normally to resume playback.
If playback is stuck or tones are corrupted, power off the Voyager by sliding the Power switch to Off, wait 5 seconds, then power back on.
Reconnect via Poly Studio Mobile and verify normal Play/Pause behavior.
expected: No tone stacking or missing tones are heard during or after rapid Play/Pause presses. Normal single press of Play/Pause correctly toggles playback state. If audio corruption occurs, power cycling the Voyager fully restores correct tone generation and playback control.

title: FMD-003: Rapid Play/Pause Button Presses — Missing Tones on Free 60
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and confirm Free 60 earbuds are connected via Bluetooth.
Play audio media (e.g., streaming music) at moderate volume.
On the Free 60 earbud (left or right), rapidly press the Play/Pause button 12 times within 4 seconds.
Monitor audio output for missing tones — i.e., expected Play/Pause confirmation tones that do not play.
After the rapid presses, press Play/Pause once and verify the correct tone plays and media toggles.
If tones are missing or playback is unresponsive, place Free 60 earbuds in the charging case, close the lid, wait 10 seconds, then remove and reconnect.
Verify in Poly Studio Mobile that Free 60 reconnects and Play/Pause functions normally.
expected: All Play/Pause tones play correctly without any missing tones during rapid presses. After rapid input, a single press correctly toggles playback and plays the appropriate tone. If tones are lost, placing earbuds in the case and removing them restores normal tone and playback behavior.

title: FMD-004: Rapid Play/Pause Button Presses — Audio Output Integrity on Surround 85
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and ensure Surround 85 headphones are connected via Bluetooth.
Start media playback (e.g., a test track with clear audio cues) at 50% volume.
On the Surround 85 right earcup, rapidly press the Play/Pause button 20 times within 5 seconds.
Evaluate audio output for: (a) tone stacking — overlapping tones playing simultaneously, (b) missing tones — expected tones not heard, (c) audio stutter or distortion.
After rapid presses, press Play/Pause once and verify correct single tone and playback state toggle.
If audio output is corrupted, power off Surround 85 by holding the Power button for 4 seconds, wait 10 seconds, then power back on.
Reconnect via Poly Studio Mobile and verify audio integrity with normal Play/Pause.
expected: Audio output remains clear with no tone stacking, missing tones, stutter, or distortion during or after rapid Play/Pause presses. A single press after rapid input correctly toggles playback with a single clean tone. Power cycling the Surround 85 fully restores audio integrity if corruption occurs.

title: FMD-005: Rapid Play/Pause Button Presses — App Audio State Sync After Recovery
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect a Poly Bluetooth device (e.g., Sync 20, Voyager, Free 60, or Surround 85).
Play media audio from the connected phone.
On the physical device, rapidly press Play/Pause 15 times within 5 seconds to trigger potential FMD tone corruption.
Observe the Poly Studio Mobile app UI — check the Play/Pause icon state on the Now Playing or Audio Settings screen.
If audio becomes unresponsive, power off the physical device and power it back on.
Reconnect the device in Poly Studio Mobile.
Verify that the app's Play/Pause icon state matches the actual audio playback state (playing or paused).
Press Play/Pause from the app UI and verify correct audio state transition.
expected: The Poly Studio Mobile app UI correctly reflects the audio playback state after rapid button presses. After device power cycle and reconnection, the app's Play/Pause icon accurately shows the current playback state. Pressing Play/Pause from the app correctly toggles audio playback.

title: FMD-006: Rapid Play/Pause Button Presses — Device Debounce Validation on Sync 20+
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect Sync 20+ via Bluetooth.
Play streaming audio at 50% volume.
On the Sync 20+, rapidly press the Play/Pause button 25 times within 5 seconds (simulating a stuck or bouncing button scenario).
After the rapid presses, wait 2 seconds, then press Play/Pause once.
Verify that only one Play/Pause toggle occurs (not multiple toggles from debounce failure).
Check audio output for any residual tone stacking or missing tones.
If audio is corrupted, power cycle Sync 20+ by holding Power button for 2 seconds, waiting 5 seconds, and powering back on.
Reconnect and verify normal single-press Play/Pause behavior.
expected: The Sync 20+ button debounce logic prevents multiple toggles from a single intended press. After rapid presses, a single press results in exactly one Play/Pause toggle. No tone stacking or missing tones are present. Power cycling restores normal behavior if needed.

title: FMD-007: Rapid Play/Pause Button Presses — FMD Tone Recovery Without App Intervention
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect a Poly Bluetooth device (e.g., Voyager 5200).
Play media audio.
On the physical device, rapidly press Play/Pause 10 times within 3 seconds to induce FMD tone stacking or missing tones.
Do NOT use the Poly Studio Mobile app to intervene.
On the physical device only, press and hold the Play/Pause button for 5 seconds to attempt a soft reset or state clear.
Then press Play/Pause once normally.
If tones remain corrupted, power off the device using the physical Power button, wait 10 seconds, and power back on.
Verify that normal Play/Pause tone and playback behavior is restored using only physical device controls.
expected: The Poly device can recover from FMD tone corruption using only physical button controls (long press or power cycle) without requiring the Poly Studio Mobile app. After recovery, single Play/Pause press produces correct tone and toggles playback correctly.

title: FMD-008: Rapid Play/Pause Button Presses — Concurrent Call and Media Scenario
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect a Poly Bluetooth device (e.g., Surround 85).
Place an active phone call on the connected device.
While on the call, start media playback in the background (e.g., music app).
End the call — media should resume automatically.
Immediately after call ends, rapidly press Play/Pause on the physical device 10 times within 3 seconds.
Observe for tone stacking or missing tones during the rapid presses.
Press Play/Pause once and verify correct media toggle.
If audio is corrupted, power cycle the device and reconnect.
expected: No tone stacking or missing tones occur during rapid Play/Pause presses after a call-to-media transition. A single press correctly toggles media playback. The device handles the state transition from call to media without audio corruption from rapid button input.
